senses.definition: having a surface that is curved outward

examples.x: The convex mirror in the hallway made the space appear larger than it actually was.
The artist used a convex mold to create the rounded sculpture.

senses.definition: having six equal sides and six angles

examples.x: The honeycomb displayed a hexagonal arrangement of cells, efficiently utilizing space in the beehive.
The tabletop had a unique hexagonal design, adding a modern and geometric touch to the furniture.
The soccer ball featured a hexagonal pattern on its surface, creating the iconic appearance of the classic ball.
The architecture of the building incorporated hexagonal windows, allowing for both aesthetics and natural light.
The beaded snowflake had a hexagonal structure, reflecting the intricate beauty of individual ice crystals.

senses.definition: having a surface that is curved inward
having the shape of a trapezoid, a quadrilateral with one pair of parallel sides

examples.x: The school auditorium had trapezoidal seating arrangements, optimizing sightlines for a better view of the stage.
The graphic designer incorporated trapezoidal shapes into the logo, giving it a contemporary and dynamic look.
The tabletop had a trapezoidal form, enhancing the uniqueness of the custom-made furniture.
The city plaza had trapezoidal tiles in its pavement, forming an interesting and distinctive pattern throughout the pedestrian area.

senses.definition: having curved lines, forms, or structures

examples.x: The architect incorporated curvilinear windows into the building's design, allowing for a harmonious and fluid appearance.

senses.definition: having a shape with multiple straight edges and angles

examples.x: The tabletop featured a polygonal pattern, adding a geometric touch to the furniture.
The garden design incorporated polygonal paving stones, creating an interesting and structured pathway.
The playground equipment had a polygonal design, offering children a fun and visually appealing play area.
The architectural blueprint included a polygonal courtyard, enhancing the aesthetic appeal of the building.

senses.definition: having the form of a ring

examples.x: The ornate mirror had an annular frame, adding a touch of elegance to the room.
The tree's annular growth rings provided insight into its age and environmental history.
The geologist studied the annular patterns in the rock formations to understand the geological history of the area.
The treehouse incorporated annular windows, providing panoramic views of the surrounding forest.
The wedding cake had tiers with annular decorations, giving it a classic and refined appearance.

senses.definition: resembling an ellipse or an oval-shaped object

examples.x: The gemstone had an ellipsoidal cut, enhancing its brilliance with a unique oval shape.
The elliptical orbit of the comet was represented by an ellipsoidal trajectory on the astronomical chart.
The designer created an ellipsoidal vase, combining symmetry and elegance in its oval form.
The architect incorporated an ellipsoidal window design, allowing natural light to fill the room with a soft glow.
The elliptical table in the dining room had a sleek and modern ellipsoidal design, complementing the overall aesthetic.

senses.definition: resembling a curve that is U-shaped or bowl-shaped

examples.x: The satellite dish on the rooftop had a parabolic surface, ensuring optimal reception of television signals.
The mathematician explored the properties of a parabolic curve, studying its symmetrical and reflective characteristics.
The amusement park ride featured a parabolic motion, providing an exhilarating experience as riders swung back and forth in a U-shaped trajectory.

senses.definition: having the shape or characteristics of an octagon, which is a polygon with eight sides and eight angles

examples.x: The table in the conference room had an octagonal surface, providing ample space for discussions.
The medieval tower had an octagonal design, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.
The traffic sign indicated an upcoming intersection with an octagonal shape, signaling drivers to stop.
The chessboard was crafted with an octagonal pattern, adding a unique twist to the classic game.
The stained glass window in the church depicted religious scenes within an octagonal frame.

senses.definition: characterized by or resembling a tetrahedron, which has four triangular faces

examples.x: The chemist explained that a methane molecule has a tetrahedral geometry, with the carbon atom at the center.
The artist's sculpture featured a series of interlocking tetrahedral shapes, creating a complex and fascinating design.
The tetrahedral arrangement of atoms in the crystal lattice contributes to its unique properties.
In geometry class, they learned how to calculate the angles between the faces of a tetrahedral pyramid.
The building's tetrahedral structure provided both aesthetic appeal and structural stability.

senses.definition: having the shape of a pentagon, which is characterized by five straight sides and five angles

examples.x: The decorative tiles on the patio had a pentagonal pattern, creating an interesting geometric design.
The military badge featured a pentagonal emblem, symbolizing strength and unity.
The clock face in the town square was designed with a pentagonal frame, enhancing its visual appeal.
The decorative mirror on the wall had a pentagonal frame, adding a touch of sophistication to the room.
The architectural blueprint outlined a building with a pentagonal floor plan, maximizing interior space.

senses.definition: having the form or characteristics of a cone, which is a three-dimensional geometric shape with a circular base tapering to a point at the apex

examples.x: The birthday hat was cone-shaped, adorned with colorful patterns for a festive look.
The ice cream was served in a cone-shaped wafer, adding a delightful crunch to each bite.
The pine trees in the forest often had a cone-shaped crown, characteristic of their species.
The wizard's hat in the costume shop was tall and cone-shaped, perfect for a magical ensemble.
The volcano emitted ash in a cone-shaped plume during the eruption.

senses.definition: having a spiral or wound shape, often forming a series of loops or turns

examples.x: The gymnast executed a perfect flip, displaying a coiled form in mid-air before landing gracefully.
The climber secured the rope with a coiled knot before ascending the rock face.

senses.definition: having the shape or characteristics of a tube

examples.x: The exhaust system of the car included tubular components for efficient gas flow.
The modern chair had tubular metal legs, contributing to its sleek and minimalist appearance.
The flowers in the garden had tubular petals, attracting hummingbirds with their unique shape.
The playground equipment included tubular slides, offering children an exciting play experience.
The telescope had a tubular design, allowing for easy adjustment and focus.

senses.definition: resembling a structure with a polygonal base and triangular sides, often tapering to a point at the apex
resembling a doughnut or a ring-shaped object

examples.x: The toroidal doughnut was freshly baked and glazed, tempting everyone with its sweet aroma.
The toroidal pool design in the resort's courtyard provided a unique and visually appealing place for relaxation.
The modern sculpture in the park had a toroidal shape, creating an interesting focal point for visitors.
The coffee shop offered toroidal bagels, a popular choice for those looking for a different twist on the traditional.
The tire company introduced a new line of toroidal tires, promising improved performance and durability.

senses.definition: having a rounded, swollen, or bulb-shaped form

examples.x: The tulip bulbs were bulbous, ready to bloom into vibrant flowers in the spring.
The cartoon character had a bulbous nose, adding a humorous touch to its appearance.
The cactus had a bulbous base, serving as a water storage reservoir in arid environments.
The chef prepared a dish with bulbous mushrooms, enhancing the texture and flavor.
The raindrops collected on the leaves, forming bulbous droplets after the storm.

senses.definition: a rectangular figure that has unequal adjacent sides with arched angles

examples.x: The rug in the living room was an attractive oblong with intricate patterns.

senses.definition: a curved shape with narrow points at the ends that appears wider in the middle, like the shape of the moon in its first and last quarters

examples.x: The moon hung low in the sky, its slender crescent casting a gentle light.
The street was named Crescent Avenue because it curved in a perfect crescent shape.
She watched the crescent slowly grow each night as the moon waxed towards fullness.
The ancient coin featured a crescent and star, symbols of the civilization that minted it.
The crescent of the new moon was barely visible against the twilight sky.
